agave.description: The Validator for the Solana ecosystem. The majority of development tools are built on top of this codebase.

anchor.description: The most popular development framework on Solana.

quasar.description: The fastest development framework on Solana.

pinocchio.description: The most popular lightweight development frameowrk on Solana.

solana_explorer.description: The most popular transaction explorer on Solana. Uses RPC methods to display transaction data.

drift_protocol_v2.description: On-chain perpetuals dex with multiple liquidity mechanisms.

litesvm.description: Fast and lightweight library for testing Solana programs. Most popular for unit testing.

surfpool.description: Drop-in replacement for `solana-test-validator`, capable of running with real mainnet state. Most popular for end-to-end testing.

solana_web3_js.description: Legacy (1.x) JavaScript/TypeScript client SDK for RPC, transactions, and accounts. Still widely used in existing apps.

solana_kit.description: Modern modular JavaScript SDK (@solana/kit), the successor to web3.js 2.x. Recommended for new client development.

solana_sdk.description: Rust SDK for on-chain program and client development; home of the core Solana types and syscalls used by Agave.

spl_token.description: SPL Token program and clients—the standard fungible-token interface on Solana.

token_2022.description: Token-2022 (Token Extensions) program and clients for transfer fees, hooks, and other extensions.

mpl_token_metadata.description: Metaplex Token Metadata program for attaching name, URI, and royalty data to fungible and non-fungible tokens.

mollusk.description: Lightweight Anza SVM test harness for fast, deterministic on-chain program unit tests.

phoenix.description: On-chain central limit order book that atomically settles trades.

openbook_v2.description: OpenBook v2 CLOB program and TypeScript client—the successor to Serum on Solana.

raydium_amm.description: Raydium constant-product AMM integrated with OpenBook for hybrid pool and order-book liquidity.

orca_whirlpools.description: Orca concentrated-liquidity AMM (Whirlpools)—reference for CLMM math and pool architecture on Solana.
